sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Lower back pain with left leg radiculopathy.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports lower back pain that began four days ago after bending over to pick up an item in the yard. He describes the pain as sharp and radiating down his left leg. He attributes the pain to the physical exertion of yard work, including carrying items. He reports no recent trauma. He has occasionally experienced back pain in the past. He has been taking ibuprofen with some relief. He reports his wife makes cookies and he continues to eat them, despite trying to reduce carbohydrate intake. He checks his blood sugar two or three times a week, with readings typically in the 120-140 range. He takes metformin 500 mg twice daily.
Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation in the left paraspinal area. Straight leg raise is negative bilaterally. Neurological exam is intact. There is one plus edema of the lower extremities. Lung exam is clear. Heart exam reveals a two over six systolic ejection murmur, which is stable. No calf tenderness or signs of deep vein thrombosis.
Assessment
Musculoskeletal strain of the lumbar paraspinal muscles is the most likely diagnosis. The patient's history of diabetes and hypertension are present.
Plan
medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation in the left paraspinal area
musculoskeletal strain of the lumbar paraspinal muscles

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Order a hemoglobin A1c to assess glycemic control. Continue metformin 500 mg twice daily. Start naproxen 500 mg twice daily and flexeril 10 mg twice daily for musculoskeletal pain. Refer to physical therapy for evaluation and treatment. Order a chest x-ray and CBC/BMP. Initiate hydrochlorothiazide 10 mg daily for edema, titrating as needed. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess response to treatment and adjust medications as necessary.

medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation in the left paraspinal area
musculoskeletal strain of the lumbar paraspinal muscles
